entity.summary: Jakarta-based Indonesian cryptocurrency exchange operated by PT Triniti Investama Berkat from 2020. FTX became its controlling shareholder in 2022, and the exchange interface now displays a shareholder-closure notice with deposits closed and only a limited withdrawal window, but no reliable dated final shutdown marker has been recovered.
entity.notes: The public homepage still describes investment and trading services, while the member exchange interface displays a closure notice tied to shareholder FTX, states that deposits are closed, and asks users to withdraw remaining balances within one month. HEI therefore uses inactive rather than active or dead. No death date is assigned because the notice lacks a reliable publication date and final effective-closure date.

events.title: Bitocto began serving Indonesian crypto traders
events.description: Bitocto began operating in 2020 through PT Triniti Investama Berkat as an Indonesian rupiah-based cryptocurrency investment and trading platform.
events.notes: Month-level operating marker based on company incorporation and contemporary exchange-profile reporting.

events.title: FTX majority ownership of Bitocto became public
events.description: Bankruptcy and corporate records revealed that FTX controlled approximately 99% of Bitocto, placing the Indonesian exchange within the FTX Dotcom corporate group.
events.notes: This is a public-confirmation marker rather than a claimed legal acquisition-closing date. Indonesian corporate changes had been documented earlier in 2022.

evidence.title: Bitocto company profile
evidence.notes: Company-controlled profile identifies Bitocto as a Jakarta exchange founded in 2020 and operated as a regulated Indonesian platform.

evidence.title: Review Exchange Kripto Bitocto
evidence.notes: Exchange review states that PT Triniti Investama Berkat had served Indonesian users through Bitocto since May 2020.

evidence.title: FTX Ternyata Jadi Pemegang Saham Mayoritas di Bitocto
evidence.notes: Reporting based on bankruptcy and Indonesian corporate records documents FTX's majority ownership and earlier corporate filings.

evidence.title: Saham Mayoritas Dimiliki FTX, Bagaimana Nasib Bitocto?
evidence.notes: Independent Indonesian reporting confirms that Bitocto appeared in the FTX Dotcom silo and that FTX held the controlling interest.

evidence.title: Bitocto shareholder closure and withdrawal notice
evidence.notes: The exchange interface states that shareholder FTX decided to close the company, deposits are closed, and withdrawals remain available only for a one-month period. The notice does not provide a reliable publication or final-closure date.

evidence.title: Bitocto official platform homepage
evidence.notes: The public homepage remains reachable and identifies PT Triniti Investama Berkat, creating a mixed public state that supports inactive rather than a fully dated dead classification.
